intent: Check whether the candidate can define NestJS clearly and explain the real problem it solves compared with writing an unstructured Express application.
primaryQuestion: What is NestJS, and what problem does it solve that plain Express does not solve by itself?

expectedSignals.label: Defines NestJS as a progressive Node.js server-side framework for building scalable applications.
Explains that Express is intentionally minimal, while Nest adds an application architecture around modules, controllers, providers, dependency injection, decorators, and lifecycle hooks.
States that Nest does not replace the HTTP engine entirely; it commonly runs on top of Express or Fastify through adapters.
Connects the value of NestJS to maintainability, testability, consistency, and team-scale code organization rather than only saying it is 'more structured'.
Mentions that Express can solve the same business problems, but the team must design and enforce the architecture itself.

redFlags: Says NestJS is just Express with TypeScript and decorators.
Claims Express cannot build large applications or cannot use dependency injection at all.
Cannot name concrete Nest building blocks such as modules, controllers, providers, or DI.
Describes NestJS only as an ORM, REST library, or MVC template generator.

scoringHints.description: Gives a crisp definition, separates framework architecture from HTTP engine, and explains concrete maintainability and team-scale benefits over raw Express.
Understands that NestJS adds structure on top of Express/Fastify but misses one important mechanism such as DI, modules, or adapter abstraction.
Knows NestJS is a backend framework but explains the difference from Express only with vague words like 'cleaner' or 'enterprise'.
Answer does not show a practical understanding of NestJS or Express responsibilities.

followUps.question: Can you name three concrete NestJS features that create structure beyond what Express gives you by default?
followUps.purpose: Force the answer from generic framework preference into specific architecture mechanisms.

followUps.question: What would you lose if you built the same API in plain Express with no agreed project conventions?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can connect framework structure to real maintenance risks.

en.title: What NestJS solves over plain Express
en.displayQuestion: What is NestJS, and what problem does it solve that plain Express does not solve by itself?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can explain NestJS as an architectural framework, not just a routing library.
guidance: Define NestJS in one or two precise sentences before comparing it with Express.
Mention modules, controllers, providers, dependency injection, decorators, and lifecycle as concrete mechanisms.
Explain that Express is flexible and minimal, while Nest provides conventions that help larger teams keep code consistent.
Be clear that Express can still be used for large systems if the team builds its own structure.
commonMistakes: Calling NestJS simply 'Express with TypeScript'.
Saying Express is unsuitable for production or large systems.
Listing features without explaining the architectural problem they solve.
Confusing NestJS with an ORM or a database framework.

vi.title: NestJS giải quyết gì so với Express thuần
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS là gì, và nó giải quyết vấn đề gì mà Express thuần không tự giải quyết sẵn?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có giải thích được NestJS như một framework kiến trúc, không chỉ là thư viện routing.
guidance: Định nghĩa NestJS gọn và chính xác trước khi so sánh với Express.
Nhắc tới module, controller, provider, dependency injection, decorator và lifecycle như các cơ chế cụ thể.
Giải thích Express rất linh hoạt và tối giản, còn Nest cung cấp convention giúp team lớn giữ code nhất quán.
Nói rõ Express vẫn có thể dùng cho hệ thống lớn nếu team tự xây và enforce kiến trúc.
commonMistakes: Gọi NestJS đơn giản là 'Express có TypeScript'.
Nói Express không phù hợp production hoặc hệ thống lớn.
Liệt kê feature mà không giải thích vấn đề kiến trúc phía sau.
Nhầm NestJS với ORM hoặc framework database.

ja.title: NestJS が plain Express より解決すること
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS とは何で、plain Express だけでは標準で解決しないどのような問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS を単なる routing library ではなく、architecture framework として説明できるか確認する。
guidance: Express と比較する前に、NestJS を一、二文で正確に定義する。
modules、controllers、providers、dependency injection、decorators、lifecycle など具体的な仕組みに触れる。
Express は柔軟で最小限、Nest は大きな team で一貫した code を保つ convention を提供することを説明する。
Express でも team が自分たちで構造を設計し enforce すれば大規模 system を作れる点も明確にする。
commonMistakes: NestJS を単に 'TypeScript 付き Express' と呼ぶ。
Express は production や大規模 system に向かないと言い切る。
解決する architecture 問題を説明せず feature だけ列挙する。
NestJS を ORM や database framework と混同する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Documentation overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for the official framework definition, Angular-inspired architecture, and conventional project structure.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for modules, providers, imports, exports, and application graph concepts.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: First steps
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express/Fastify platform foundation and default platform details.

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands the underlying HTTP platforms supported by NestJS and the adapter abstraction.
primaryQuestion: Which HTTP platforms can NestJS run on top of, and what is the default platform?

expectedSignals.label: States that NestJS supports Express and Fastify out of the box through platform packages and adapters.
Identifies Express via @nestjs/platform-express as the default platform in a normal Nest application.
Explains that adapters wrap the underlying HTTP server library so most application code stays platform-agnostic.
Mentions that the underlying request and response objects can still be accessed, but doing so may couple code to a specific platform.
Recognizes that other HTTP frameworks require a compatible adapter rather than being automatically supported.

redFlags: Claims NestJS is a completely separate HTTP server unrelated to Express or Fastify.
Says Fastify is always the default or Express is the only possible platform.
Cannot explain what an adapter does.
Assumes all Express middleware and Fastify plugins are interchangeable without platform-specific considerations.

scoringHints.description: Correctly names Express and Fastify, default Express platform, adapter role, and platform-coupling risks.
Knows Express and Fastify are supported but has a shallow explanation of adapters or default platform behavior.
Has heard of Express/Fastify integration but cannot describe how Nest abstracts the HTTP platform.
Answer is incorrect about the HTTP platform foundation.

followUps.question: What package would you install if you wanted to run a Nest application on Fastify instead of the default Express platform?
followUps.purpose: Check practical adapter knowledge.

followUps.question: What code patterns might make a Nest app harder to switch from Express to Fastify later?
followUps.purpose: Probe platform-agnostic design awareness.

en.title: NestJS HTTP platform adapters
en.displayQuestion: Which HTTP platforms can NestJS run on top of, and what is the default platform?
en.displayIntent: Verify understanding of Express, Fastify, and the Nest adapter layer.
guidance: Name Express and Fastify as out-of-the-box HTTP platforms.
State that @nestjs/platform-express is used by default.
Explain what an HTTP adapter wraps and why most application code can stay platform-agnostic.
Mention the risk of directly using platform-specific request/response APIs.
commonMistakes: Thinking NestJS has no underlying HTTP library.
Assuming Fastify is the default platform.
Treating Express middleware and Fastify plugins as drop-in equivalent everywhere.
Ignoring platform coupling caused by direct response handling.

vi.title: HTTP platform adapter trong NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS có thể chạy trên những HTTP platform nào, và platform mặc định là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Xác minh hiểu biết về Express, Fastify và lớp adapter của Nest.
guidance: Nêu Express và Fastify là hai HTTP platform được hỗ trợ sẵn.
Nói rõ @nestjs/platform-express là platform mặc định.
Giải thích HTTP adapter wrap gì và vì sao phần lớn code application có thể platform-agnostic.
Nhắc tới rủi ro khi dùng trực tiếp API request/response đặc thù của platform.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ NestJS không dùng HTTP library bên dưới.
Cho rằng Fastify là platform mặc định.
Xem Express middleware và Fastify plugin là tương đương ở mọi chỗ.
Bỏ qua coupling do xử lý response theo API riêng của platform.

ja.title: NestJS の HTTP platform adapter
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS はどの HTTP platform 上で動作でき、default platform は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express、Fastify、Nest adapter layer の理解を確認する。
guidance: Express と Fastify が out-of-the-box の HTTP platform であることを述べる。
@nestjs/platform-express が default platform であることを明確にする。
HTTP adapter が何を wrap し、なぜ多くの application code を platform-agnostic にできるか説明する。
platform-specific な request/response API を直接使う risk に触れる。
commonMistakes: NestJS の下に HTTP library がないと思い込む。
Fastify が default platform だと考える。
Express middleware と Fastify plugin が常にそのまま置き換え可能だと扱う。
direct response handling による platform coupling を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: First steps
sourceReferences.note: Used for default platform and out-of-the-box Express/Fastify support.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: HTTP adapter FAQ
sourceReferences.note: Used for the adapter abstraction and native HTTP server wrapping.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Performance
sourceReferences.note: Used for Fastify adapter setup and performance-oriented platform selection.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can compare NestJS and Express without oversimplifying either framework and can choose based on team and product constraints.
primaryQuestion: How is NestJS different from Express, and when would you choose one over the other?

expectedSignals.label: Compares Express as a minimal, unopinionated HTTP framework with NestJS as a higher-level application framework with conventions.
Names concrete Nest mechanisms: modules, DI container, controllers, providers, pipes, guards, interceptors, filters, testing utilities, and lifecycle hooks.
Chooses Express for small services, prototypes, highly custom stacks, or teams that already have strong internal conventions.
Chooses NestJS for larger teams, long-lived APIs, modular domains, multiple transports, consistent testing, and shared architectural patterns.
Acknowledges trade-offs: Nest adds learning curve, abstraction, bootstrapping overhead, and convention constraints.

redFlags: Says NestJS is always better than Express or Express is obsolete.
Says Express cannot use TypeScript, validation, DI, or project structure.
Chooses purely based on benchmark speed without discussing maintainability or team constraints.
Cannot articulate any downside of NestJS.

scoringHints.description: Balances architecture, team scale, product lifetime, customization, learning curve, and operational constraints when comparing Express and NestJS.
Gives a mostly accurate comparison but under-explains either Express strengths or NestJS trade-offs.
Frames the choice as personal taste or popularity rather than a technical and organizational trade-off.
Comparison is factually wrong or too generic to evaluate framework judgment.

followUps.question: Give me one case where Express would be the better choice even if the team already knows NestJS.
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can recognize legitimate reasons not to use NestJS.

followUps.question: What project signals would make you switch from a simple Express service to NestJS?
followUps.purpose: Probe practical decision thresholds.

en.title: NestJS versus Express decision
en.displayQuestion: How is NestJS different from Express, and when would you choose one over the other?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate practical trade-off reasoning between a minimal HTTP framework and a structured application framework.
guidance: Compare architecture, not only syntax.
Discuss small services, prototypes, custom stacks, large teams, modular domains, and long-lived APIs.
Name real NestJS mechanisms that Express does not impose by default.
Include NestJS costs such as learning curve, abstraction, and convention constraints.
commonMistakes: Saying NestJS is simply better in all cases.
Claiming Express cannot be structured or cannot use TypeScript.
Choosing only by request throughput benchmarks.
Ignoring team size, codebase lifetime, and convention enforcement.

vi.title: Quyết định chọn NestJS hay Express
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS và Express khác nhau như thế nào, và khi nào bạn chọn framework nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá tư duy trade-off thực tế giữa HTTP framework tối giản và application framework có cấu trúc.
guidance: So sánh về kiến trúc, không chỉ syntax.
Nói về service nhỏ, prototype, stack tùy biến, team lớn, domain dạng module và API sống lâu.
Nêu các cơ chế NestJS cụ thể mà Express không áp đặt sẵn.
Bao gồm chi phí của NestJS như learning curve, abstraction và ràng buộc convention.
commonMistakes: Nói NestJS luôn tốt hơn trong mọi trường hợp.
Claim Express không thể có structure hoặc không thể dùng TypeScript.
Chọn chỉ dựa trên benchmark request throughput.
Bỏ qua team size, tuổi thọ codebase và việc enforce convention.

ja.title: NestJS と Express の選択判断
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS と Express はどう違い、どのような場合にどちらを選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: 最小限の HTTP framework と構造化された application framework の実践的な trade-off reasoning を評価する。
guidance: syntax だけでなく architecture を比較する。
small service、prototype、custom stack、大きな team、modular domain、長期運用 API について話す。
Express が default では強制しない NestJS の具体的な仕組みを挙げる。
learning curve、abstraction、convention constraints など NestJS の cost も含める。
commonMistakes: NestJS がすべての場面で単純に優れていると言う。
Express は structure を作れない、TypeScript を使えないと主張する。
request throughput benchmark だけで選ぶ。
team size、codebase lifetime、convention enforcement を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Documentation overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for NestJS framework scope and conventional application structure.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for modular architecture and dependency graph behavior.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for controller routing and decorator-based metadata.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands why NestJS is opinionated and can evaluate the benefits and costs of framework conventions.
primaryQuestion: Why is NestJS designed to be opinionated, and what are the advantages and disadvantages of that?

expectedSignals.label: Explains opinionated design as providing a default way to structure modules, controllers, providers, dependency injection, and cross-cutting concerns.
Identifies benefits such as consistency, onboarding speed, testability, shared mental model, tooling support, and easier refactoring across a large codebase.
Identifies costs such as steeper learning curve, more boilerplate for small apps, framework lock-in, abstraction overhead, and occasional friction with non-standard designs.
Connects opinionated structure to team-scale software development rather than treating it as only a style preference.
States that good teams still need local conventions for domain boundaries, naming, testing, and module organization.

redFlags: Describes opinionated as meaning inflexible or impossible to customize.
Only lists benefits and cannot name realistic downsides.
Only complains about boilerplate and misses why conventions help teams.
Cannot connect NestJS conventions to modules, DI, testing, or cross-cutting concerns.

scoringHints.description: Explains the design philosophy and gives balanced pros and cons tied to team scale, maintainability, and real project constraints.
Understands that conventions help consistency but misses deeper costs or the need for local architecture decisions.
Frames opinionated design as only good or only bad without trade-off reasoning.
Answer does not demonstrate understanding of NestJS's architectural design philosophy.

followUps.question: What is one NestJS convention that helps large teams but might feel heavy in a small service?
followUps.purpose: Ground the trade-off in a concrete framework convention.

followUps.question: Where would you still create team-specific conventions on top of NestJS defaults?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands framework conventions are not a complete architecture.

en.title: Opinionated framework trade-offs
en.displayQuestion: Why is NestJS designed to be opinionated, and what are the advantages and disadvantages of that?
en.displayIntent: Assess balanced reasoning about conventions, maintainability, and framework cost.
guidance: Define what opinionated means in the context of NestJS project structure.
Use concrete examples such as modules, providers, controllers, DI, pipes, guards, and interceptors.
Explain both team-scale benefits and costs for smaller or highly custom projects.
Mention that NestJS conventions still need thoughtful domain boundaries and local standards.
commonMistakes: Equating opinionated design with no flexibility.
Only saying it is good because code is clean.
Only saying it is bad because it has boilerplate.
Ignoring the organizational reason conventions exist.

vi.title: Trade-off của framework opinionated
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao NestJS được thiết kế theo hướng opinionated, và ưu nhược điểm của điều đó là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng lập luận cân bằng về convention, maintainability và chi phí framework.
guidance: Định nghĩa opinionated trong bối cảnh cấu trúc project NestJS.
Dùng ví dụ cụ thể như module, provider, controller, DI, pipe, guard và interceptor.
Giải thích cả lợi ích cho team lớn lẫn chi phí với project nhỏ hoặc yêu cầu tùy biến cao.
Nhắc rằng convention của NestJS vẫn cần domain boundary và tiêu chuẩn nội bộ được thiết kế tốt.
commonMistakes: Đồng nhất opinionated với không thể linh hoạt.
Chỉ nói tốt vì code sạch hơn.
Chỉ nói xấu vì có boilerplate.
Bỏ qua lý do tổ chức phía sau convention.

ja.title: opinionated framework の trade-off
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS はなぜ opinionated に設計されており、その利点と欠点は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: convention、maintainability、framework cost についてバランスよく考えられるか評価する。
guidance: NestJS project structure の文脈で opinionated の意味を定義する。
modules、providers、controllers、DI、pipes、guards、interceptors など具体例を使う。
大きな team での benefits と、小規模または高度に custom な project での cost の両方を説明する。
NestJS conventions があっても、domain boundaries と local standards の設計が必要である点に触れる。
commonMistakes: opinionated design を柔軟性がないことと同一視する。
code が clean になるから良い、だけで終わる。
boilerplate があるから悪い、だけで終わる。
convention が存在する organizational reason を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Documentation overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for conventional project structure and Angular-inspired architecture.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for module organization and application graph concepts.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Providers
sourceReferences.note: Used for provider and DI responsibilities.

intent: Check whether the candidate knows that NestJS architecture is heavily inspired by Angular and can connect that inspiration to concrete backend structure.
primaryQuestion: Which framework inspired NestJS, and how does that influence its structure?

expectedSignals.label: States that NestJS architecture is heavily inspired by Angular.
Connects the influence to decorators, modules, dependency injection, providers, controllers, and metadata-driven configuration.
Explains that Nest applies these ideas to server-side concerns such as routing, validation, authorization, lifecycle, and microservice transports.
Mentions that the similarity helps developers reason about application composition and dependency boundaries.
Avoids implying that NestJS is Angular running on the server or that it shares the same runtime model.

redFlags: Names React, Express, or Spring as the primary inspiration without mentioning Angular.
Says NestJS is literally Angular for backend with no differences.
Cannot connect Angular inspiration to modules, decorators, or dependency injection.
Treats decorators as only syntax sugar with no metadata role.

scoringHints.description: Correctly names Angular and explains how modules, DI, decorators, and metadata shape the NestJS backend structure.
Names Angular and some structural similarities but misses how those ideas map to server-side application design.
Recognizes surface syntax but cannot explain the architecture influence.
Answer is wrong or too vague about NestJS origins and structure.

followUps.question: Can you map Angular-like concepts to NestJS concepts, for example module, provider, and decorator?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands more than trivia.

followUps.question: How does decorator metadata help Nest build routes or resolve dependencies?
followUps.purpose: Probe the mechanism behind the structural similarity.

en.title: Angular influence on NestJS
en.displayQuestion: Which framework inspired NestJS, and how does that influence its structure?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate can connect Angular inspiration to concrete NestJS architecture.
guidance: Name Angular directly.
Discuss modules, providers, decorators, DI, metadata, and application graph composition.
Explain how those concepts become server-side routing, validation, authorization, and lifecycle patterns.
Avoid saying NestJS is the same runtime as Angular.
commonMistakes: Only saying NestJS looks like Angular without explaining why.
Naming Express or React as the primary architectural inspiration.
Missing dependency injection and metadata.
Forgetting that NestJS targets server-side Node.js applications.

vi.title: Ảnh hưởng của Angular lên NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS lấy cảm hứng từ framework nào, và điều đó ảnh hưởng đến cấu trúc của nó như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có kết nối được nguồn cảm hứng Angular với kiến trúc NestJS cụ thể hay không.
guidance: Nêu trực tiếp Angular.
Nói về module, provider, decorator, DI, metadata và cách compose application graph.
Giải thích các concept đó trở thành pattern server-side cho routing, validation, authorization và lifecycle như thế nào.
Tránh nói NestJS có cùng runtime với Angular.
commonMistakes: Chỉ nói NestJS nhìn giống Angular mà không giải thích vì sao.
Nêu Express hoặc React là nguồn cảm hứng kiến trúc chính.
Bỏ qua dependency injection và metadata.
Quên rằng NestJS nhắm tới ứng dụng server-side Node.js.

ja.title: Angular が NestJS に与えた影響
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS はどの framework から影響を受けており、それは構造にどう反映されていますか。
ja.displayIntent: Angular からの影響を具体的な NestJS architecture に結びつけられるか確認する。
guidance: Angular を直接挙げる。
modules、providers、decorators、DI、metadata、application graph composition について話す。
それらが server-side routing、validation、authorization、lifecycle pattern にどう変わるか説明する。
NestJS が Angular と同じ runtime だとは言わない。
commonMistakes: NestJS は Angular に似ていると言うだけで理由を説明しない。
Express や React を主な architectural inspiration として挙げる。
dependency injection と metadata を見落とす。
NestJS が server-side Node.js application 向けであることを忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Documentation overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for the official statement that NestJS architecture is heavily inspired by Angular.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for module graph and provider visibility concepts.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for decorator metadata and route mapping.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands NestJS's strong TypeScript orientation while recognizing that pure JavaScript is possible.
primaryQuestion: Does NestJS require TypeScript, or can you use plain JavaScript?

expectedSignals.label: States that NestJS is built with TypeScript in mind and most examples and tooling assume TypeScript.
Recognizes that NestJS can be used with plain JavaScript, including a JavaScript starter, but it is less common.
Explains that TypeScript improves the NestJS developer experience through decorators, metadata, DTO classes, type-safe DI, and editor tooling.
Mentions that JavaScript usage may lose compile-time contracts and make DTO/model consistency harder unless compensated by runtime validation and conventions.
Avoids claiming TypeScript types perform runtime validation by themselves.

redFlags: Says NestJS cannot run without TypeScript.
Says TypeScript is only cosmetic and brings no practical value in NestJS.
Confuses compile-time TypeScript types with runtime validation.
Cannot explain why DTO classes are commonly used instead of plain interfaces for validation metadata.

scoringHints.description: Accurately explains TypeScript-first design, JavaScript possibility, and the practical effect on decorators, metadata, DTOs, validation, and maintainability.
Knows TypeScript is recommended and JavaScript is possible but misses runtime metadata or validation nuances.
Gives a yes/no answer without explaining the practical development and safety implications.
Answer is factually wrong about TypeScript or JavaScript support.

followUps.question: If a DTO is only a TypeScript interface, why would ValidationPipe not have enough runtime metadata to validate it?
followUps.purpose: Check compile-time versus runtime understanding.

followUps.question: What extra discipline would a team need if it chose NestJS with plain JavaScript?
followUps.purpose: Probe whether the candidate sees the operational cost of avoiding TypeScript.

en.title: TypeScript-first but JavaScript-capable
en.displayQuestion: Does NestJS require TypeScript, or can you use plain JavaScript?
en.displayIntent: Check practical understanding of NestJS's TypeScript-first design and JavaScript support.
guidance: Say clearly that TypeScript is strongly recommended and idiomatic, but JavaScript is possible.
Explain the role of decorators, runtime metadata, DTO classes, and tooling.
Separate compile-time type safety from runtime validation.
Discuss what a team loses or must replace when using plain JavaScript.
commonMistakes: Saying NestJS strictly requires TypeScript.
Treating TypeScript as merely optional syntax with no architecture impact.
Assuming TypeScript interfaces validate runtime payloads.
Ignoring decorator metadata and DTO class behavior.

vi.title: Ưu tiên TypeScript nhưng vẫn dùng được JavaScript
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS có bắt buộc dùng TypeScript không, hay có thể dùng JavaScript thuần?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết thực tế về thiết kế TypeScript-first và khả năng hỗ trợ JavaScript của NestJS.
guidance: Nói rõ TypeScript là cách dùng được khuyến nghị và phổ biến, nhưng JavaScript vẫn có thể dùng.
Giải thích vai trò của decorator, runtime metadata, DTO class và tooling.
Tách bạch compile-time type safety khỏi runtime validation.
Nói team mất gì hoặc phải bù gì khi dùng JavaScript thuần.
commonMistakes: Nói NestJS bắt buộc phải dùng TypeScript.
Xem TypeScript chỉ là syntax tùy chọn không ảnh hưởng kiến trúc.
Cho rằng TypeScript interface validate được runtime payload.
Bỏ qua decorator metadata và hành vi của DTO class.

ja.title: TypeScript-first だが JavaScript も可能
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS は TypeScript が必須ですか、それとも plain JavaScript でも使えますか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS の TypeScript-first design と JavaScript support を実践的に理解しているか確認する。
guidance: TypeScript が強く推奨され idiomatic だが、JavaScript も可能であることを明確に言う。
decorators、runtime metadata、DTO classes、tooling の役割を説明する。
compile-time type safety と runtime validation を分けて説明する。
plain JavaScript を使う場合に team が失うもの、または補う必要があるものを話す。
commonMistakes: NestJS は TypeScript が必須だと言い切る。
TypeScript を architecture に影響しない単なる optional syntax として扱う。
TypeScript interface が runtime payload を validate すると仮定する。
decorator metadata と DTO class behavior を無視する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for TypeScript and JavaScript starter guidance.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for TypeScript and JavaScript controller examples and decorator usage.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Validation
sourceReferences.note: Used for DTO class and runtime validation context.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can explain practical differences between NestJS on Express and NestJS on Fastify.
primaryQuestion: How is NestJS with the Fastify adapter different from NestJS with the Express adapter?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that most Nest application code remains similar because Nest abstracts the HTTP server through adapters.
States that Express is the default adapter while Fastify requires @nestjs/platform-fastify and FastifyAdapter setup.
Mentions Fastify is often selected for performance characteristics and plugin ecosystem, but the actual gain depends on application bottlenecks.
Identifies compatibility differences around middleware, plugins, request/response objects, file upload, sessions, and library-specific APIs.
Notes that directly injecting or manipulating Express response objects can reduce portability to Fastify.

redFlags: Claims switching to Fastify automatically makes every Nest app faster regardless of bottleneck.
Assumes Express middleware can be used unchanged with Fastify.
Does not know that Express is the default Nest platform.
Ignores platform-specific request and response API differences.

scoringHints.description: Explains adapter setup, code portability, performance motivation, plugin and middleware compatibility, and platform-specific coupling risks.
Understands default Express and optional Fastify but misses some compatibility or portability details.
Frames Fastify only as faster Express without understanding the adapter boundary.
Answer is too vague or incorrect about Fastify adapter behavior.

followUps.question: Before switching a production Nest app from Express to Fastify, what compatibility checks would you run?
followUps.purpose: Check migration and risk-control thinking.

followUps.question: What measurement would prove Fastify is actually helping your specific service?
followUps.purpose: Probe evidence-based performance judgment.

en.title: Express versus Fastify adapter
en.displayQuestion: How is NestJS with the Fastify adapter different from NestJS with the Express adapter?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate adapter-level trade-offs, portability, and performance judgment.
guidance: Start with the fact that Nest application structure mostly stays the same.
Mention default Express and the @nestjs/platform-fastify setup.
Discuss performance only with measurement and bottleneck context.
Cover middleware, plugins, request/response APIs, uploads, sessions, and library-specific behavior.
commonMistakes: Assuming Fastify always improves the whole application.
Ignoring Express-specific middleware or response handling.
Treating both platforms as identical under the adapter.
Skipping migration testing and production compatibility.

vi.title: Adapter Express và Fastify
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS với Fastify adapter khác gì so với NestJS với Express adapter?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá trade-off ở tầng adapter, portability và judgment về performance.
guidance: Bắt đầu bằng việc cấu trúc application Nest hầu như giữ nguyên.
Nhắc Express là mặc định và Fastify cần setup @nestjs/platform-fastify.
Nói về performance kèm measurement và context bottleneck.
Bao gồm middleware, plugin, API request/response, upload, session và hành vi phụ thuộc platform.
commonMistakes: Giả định Fastify luôn làm cả application nhanh hơn.
Bỏ qua middleware hoặc response handling đặc thù Express.
Xem hai platform hoàn toàn giống nhau bên dưới adapter.
Không nói tới migration testing và compatibility production.

ja.title: Express adapter と Fastify adapter
ja.displayQuestion: Fastify adapter を使う NestJS は Express adapter を使う NestJS と何が違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: adapter level の trade-off、portability、performance judgment を評価する。
guidance: Nest application structure はほぼ同じであることから始める。
Express が default で、Fastify は @nestjs/platform-fastify setup が必要であることを述べる。
performance は measurement と bottleneck context と一緒に語る。
middleware、plugins、request/response APIs、uploads、sessions、platform-specific behavior を扱う。
commonMistakes: Fastify が常に application 全体を速くすると仮定する。
Express-specific middleware や response handling を無視する。
adapter の下で両 platform が完全に同じだと扱う。
migration testing と production compatibility を省く。

sourceReferences.note: Used for Fastify adapter installation and setup context.

sourceReferences.note: Used for Express default and out-of-the-box platform support.

sourceReferences.note: Used for native HTTP server adapter abstraction.

intent: Check whether the candidate can identify common NestJS use cases and describe why the framework fits them.
primaryQuestion: What are the most common use cases for NestJS?

expectedSignals.label: Names REST APIs as a common NestJS use case with controllers, DTOs, validation, guards, and services.
Mentions GraphQL APIs through @nestjs/graphql and resolver-based design.
Mentions microservices with transport layers such as TCP, Redis, NATS, RabbitMQ, Kafka, MQTT, or gRPC where relevant.
Mentions WebSocket gateways for real-time features.
Includes background jobs, scheduled tasks, queues, monoliths, modular backends, and BFF/API gateway patterns as practical examples.
Connects each use case to Nest's shared module, DI, and cross-cutting concern model.

redFlags: Thinks NestJS is only for REST APIs.
Lists use cases without explaining which Nest feature supports them.
Claims NestJS automatically makes an application a microservice architecture.
Confuses GraphQL support with a separate non-Nest server.

scoringHints.description: Covers REST, GraphQL, microservices, WebSockets, jobs/queues, and modular backend patterns with feature-level explanation.
Names major use cases but gives limited detail about how NestJS supports them.
Only knows REST usage or answers with generic backend examples.
Answer does not show familiarity with NestJS application types.

followUps.question: Pick one use case besides REST and explain what NestJS component or package supports it.
followUps.purpose: Move the answer from list memorization to concrete framework knowledge.

followUps.question: In what kind of product would NestJS be a good fit even if you start with a modular monolith?
followUps.purpose: Check practical application architecture reasoning.

en.title: Common NestJS use cases
en.displayQuestion: What are the most common use cases for NestJS?
en.displayIntent: Check breadth of NestJS application scenarios and the framework features behind them.
guidance: Mention REST APIs, GraphQL APIs, microservices, WebSockets, queues, scheduled tasks, and modular backend services.
For each major use case, connect it to controllers, resolvers, gateways, transporters, modules, providers, or DI.
Avoid implying that every Nest app must be a microservice.
Use examples such as product APIs, BFFs, internal platforms, real-time apps, and background processing.
commonMistakes: Reducing NestJS to REST controllers only.
Listing buzzwords without feature mapping.
Saying NestJS automatically solves distributed-system complexity.
Forgetting queues, jobs, and WebSocket use cases.

vi.title: Use case phổ biến của NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: Các use case phổ biến nhất của NestJS là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra độ rộng hiểu biết về các kiểu ứng dụng NestJS và feature framework hỗ trợ chúng.
guidance: Nhắc tới REST API, GraphQL API, microservices, WebSocket, queue, scheduled task và modular backend service.
Với mỗi use case lớn, liên hệ tới controller, resolver, gateway, transporter, module, provider hoặc DI.
Tránh ngụ ý mọi Nest app đều phải là microservice.
Dùng ví dụ như product API, BFF, internal platform, real-time app và background processing.
commonMistakes: Thu hẹp NestJS thành chỉ REST controller.
Liệt kê buzzword mà không map với feature.
Nói NestJS tự động giải quyết toàn bộ độ phức tạp distributed system.
Quên các use case queue, job và WebSocket.

ja.title: NestJS の一般的な use case
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS の最も一般的な use case は何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS application scenario の広さと、それを支える framework features を確認する。
guidance: REST API、GraphQL API、microservices、WebSockets、queues、scheduled tasks、modular backend services に触れる。
主要 use case ごとに controllers、resolvers、gateways、transporters、modules、providers、DI と結びつける。
すべての Nest app が microservice でなければならないとは言わない。
product API、BFF、internal platform、real-time app、background processing などを例にする。
commonMistakes: NestJS を REST controllers だけに縮小して説明する。
feature mapping なしで buzzword を並べる。
NestJS が distributed-system complexity を自動的に解決すると言う。
queues、jobs、WebSocket use cases を忘れる。

sourceReferences.note: Used for overall NestJS application feature map.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: GraphQL quick start
sourceReferences.note: Used for official GraphQL integration context.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Microservices
sourceReferences.note: Used for microservice transport support and application style.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands NestJS microservice support as transport abstraction and message handling, not just a deployment buzzword.

expectedSignals.label: Explains that a Nest microservice is an application using a transport layer other than standard HTTP.
Mentions @nestjs/microservices, NestFactory.createMicroservice, and transporters such as TCP, Redis, MQTT, NATS, RabbitMQ, Kafka, and gRPC.
Distinguishes request-response messaging from event-based messaging.
Mentions message pattern handlers and the reuse of concepts such as DI, modules, pipes, guards, filters, and interceptors where applicable.
Identifies real microservice concerns Nest does not automatically solve: service boundaries, versioning, idempotency, retries, observability, schema evolution, and data consistency.

redFlags: Says using NestJS automatically creates a microservice architecture.
Cannot name any transporter or message pattern concept.
Ignores distributed-system concerns like retries, idempotency, and observability.
Confuses Nest microservices with only HTTP REST calls between services.

scoringHints.description: Explains transport abstraction, message patterns, supported transporters, reusable Nest concepts, and the distributed-system responsibilities left to the team.
Knows the package and some transporters but misses operational trade-offs or event versus request-response distinction.
Uses microservice language but cannot explain how NestJS implements messaging.
Answer is too generic to validate NestJS microservice knowledge.

followUps.question: What does NestJS make easier in a microservice setup, and what does it still leave to your architecture decisions?
followUps.purpose: Separate framework support from distributed-system design responsibility.

followUps.question: When would you choose Kafka or RabbitMQ transport instead of plain HTTP between Nest services?
followUps.purpose: Probe transport-choice reasoning.

en.title: NestJS microservice support
en.displayIntent: Evaluate understanding of NestJS transport abstraction and distributed-system boundaries.
guidance: Mention @nestjs/microservices, createMicroservice, transporters, and message patterns.
Distinguish request-response and event-based messaging.
Explain which Nest concepts are reused across transports.
State clearly what NestJS does not automatically solve, such as service boundaries and data consistency.
commonMistakes: Equating microservices with multiple HTTP controllers.
Thinking NestJS solves idempotency, retries, and schema evolution automatically.
Forgetting transport-specific reliability and delivery semantics.
Ignoring observability and operational ownership.

vi.title: Hỗ trợ microservice trong NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS hỗ trợ Microservices như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá hiểu biết về transport abstraction của NestJS và ranh giới trách nhiệm trong distributed system.
guidance: Nhắc tới @nestjs/microservices, createMicroservice, transporter và message pattern.
Phân biệt request-response và event-based messaging.
Giải thích các concept Nest nào được reuse qua nhiều transport.
Nói rõ NestJS không tự giải quyết service boundary, data consistency và các vấn đề distributed system.
commonMistakes: Đồng nhất microservice với nhiều HTTP controller.
Nghĩ NestJS tự xử lý idempotency, retry và schema evolution.
Quên reliability và delivery semantic phụ thuộc transport.
Bỏ qua observability và operational ownership.

ja.title: NestJS の microservice support
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS は microservices をどのようにサポートしていますか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS の transport abstraction と distributed-system boundary の理解を評価する。
guidance: @nestjs/microservices、createMicroservice、transporters、message patterns に触れる。
request-response と event-based messaging を区別する。
どの Nest concepts が transport をまたいで reuse されるか説明する。
service boundaries や data consistency など、NestJS が自動で解決しないことを明確に述べる。
commonMistakes: microservices を複数の HTTP controllers と同一視する。
NestJS が idempotency、retries、schema evolution を自動解決すると考える。
transport-specific reliability と delivery semantics を忘れる。
observability と operational ownership を無視する。

sourceReferences.note: Used for official microservice definition, createMicroservice, transporters, and message styles.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Custom transporters
sourceReferences.note: Used for transport abstraction and extension context.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Platform agnosticism
sourceReferences.note: Used for reusing components across HTTP, microservices, and WebSockets.

intent: Assess framework selection judgment between NestJS and a standalone Fastify application.
primaryQuestion: Compared with writing standalone Fastify, when would you choose NestJS instead?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that Fastify standalone is a fast, flexible HTTP framework with its own plugin model and low overhead.
Chooses standalone Fastify for small APIs, edge services, performance-sensitive minimal handlers, or teams wanting full control over architecture.
Chooses NestJS when the value comes from modules, DI, consistent cross-cutting concerns, testing patterns, multi-transport support, and a shared team architecture.
Mentions that NestJS can still use Fastify underneath, so the choice is not simply Fastify performance versus NestJS.
Identifies decision signals such as team size, domain complexity, expected lifetime, number of integrations, need for GraphQL/microservices/WebSockets, and governance requirements.
Calls out measurement: performance should be tested against the real workload before making a framework decision.

redFlags: Says NestJS is chosen only because it is more popular or enterprise.
Treats NestJS and Fastify as mutually exclusive when Nest can run on Fastify.
Ignores the plugin and simplicity strengths of standalone Fastify.
Cannot name any NestJS capability that matters beyond routing.

scoringHints.description: Compares architecture, extensibility, team governance, runtime performance, plugin ecosystem, and long-term maintainability with workload-based measurement.
Gives a good selection rule but misses either Fastify strengths or the fact that Nest can use Fastify underneath.
Chooses based on general preference without concrete project signals.
Answer does not show credible framework selection judgment.

followUps.question: What is one real advantage of standalone Fastify that NestJS may partially hide or make heavier?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can argue both sides fairly.

followUps.question: What benchmark or production signal would you use before making performance a deciding factor?
followUps.purpose: Validate evidence-based performance decision making.

en.title: NestJS versus standalone Fastify
en.displayQuestion: Compared with writing standalone Fastify, when would you choose NestJS instead?
en.displayIntent: Assess senior framework selection judgment beyond raw performance claims.
guidance: Explain that NestJS can run on Fastify, so compare architecture and governance as well as runtime behavior.
Name cases where standalone Fastify is simpler or more direct.
Name cases where NestJS's modules, DI, testing, cross-cutting concerns, and multi-transport model pay off.
Use real decision signals such as team size, domain complexity, product lifetime, and observability needs.
commonMistakes: Framing the choice as Fastify speed versus NestJS structure only.
Ignoring standalone Fastify's plugin model and simplicity.
Choosing NestJS for vague enterprise branding.
Skipping measurement when discussing performance.

vi.title: NestJS so với Fastify thuần
vi.displayQuestion: So với viết Fastify standalone, khi nào bạn chọn NestJS?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá framework selection judgment ở mức senior, vượt khỏi claim performance chung chung.
guidance: Giải thích NestJS có thể chạy trên Fastify, nên cần so sánh cả architecture, governance và runtime behavior.
Nêu trường hợp Fastify standalone đơn giản hoặc trực tiếp hơn.
Nêu trường hợp module, DI, testing, cross-cutting concern và multi-transport model của NestJS đáng giá.
Dùng tín hiệu quyết định thực tế như team size, domain complexity, product lifetime và nhu cầu observability.
commonMistakes: Đóng khung lựa chọn thành Fastify nhanh còn NestJS có structure.
Bỏ qua plugin model và sự đơn giản của Fastify standalone.
Chọn NestJS vì branding enterprise mơ hồ.
Không nói tới measurement khi bàn performance.

ja.title: NestJS と standalone Fastify
ja.displayQuestion: standalone Fastify で書く場合と比べて、どのようなときに NestJS を選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: raw performance claim を超えた senior framework selection judgment を評価する。
guidance: NestJS は Fastify 上で動けるため、runtime behavior だけでなく architecture と governance も比較する。
standalone Fastify がより simple または direct になる場面を挙げる。
NestJS の modules、DI、testing、cross-cutting concerns、multi-transport model が効く場面を挙げる。
team size、domain complexity、product lifetime、observability needs など実際の decision signals を使う。
commonMistakes: Fastify は速く NestJS は構造化、というだけで選択を語る。
standalone Fastify の plugin model と simplicity を無視する。
曖昧な enterprise branding で NestJS を選ぶ。
performance を語るときに measurement を省く。

sourceReferences.note: Used for Fastify adapter setup and performance context.

sourceReferences.note: Used for reusing Nest components across different underlying platforms.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can compare NestJS with traditional MVC or enterprise backend frameworks without relying on shallow language comparisons.
primaryQuestion: What is the biggest difference between NestJS and traditional MVC frameworks such as Laravel or Spring Boot?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that NestJS is a Node.js framework organized around modules, controllers, providers, DI, decorators, and transport abstractions rather than only classic MVC folders.
Compares the ecosystem and runtime model: Node.js event loop and TypeScript/JavaScript versus PHP or JVM-based runtimes.
Mentions that Spring Boot is also opinionated and DI-heavy, so the difference is not simply 'Nest has DI and Spring does not'.
Mentions that Laravel includes many batteries-included conventions, while Nest is often more modular and package-composed around Node ecosystem choices.
Highlights Nest's platform-agnostic approach across HTTP, WebSockets, GraphQL, and microservices with reusable application components.
Avoids unfair claims that one style is categorically more scalable or enterprise-ready.

redFlags: Says NestJS is MVC and leaves it at that.
Claims Spring Boot does not have DI or decorators/annotations-like metadata concepts.
Says Laravel or Spring cannot build APIs, microservices, or scalable systems.
Compares only programming language syntax and ignores runtime, ecosystem, and architecture.

scoringHints.description: Compares architecture model, runtime, ecosystem, opinionated defaults, package composition, and transport/platform scope with fair nuance.
Gives a reasonable comparison but misses important nuance about Spring Boot or Laravel similarities.
Relies on stereotypes such as Node is lightweight or Spring is enterprise without architectural substance.
Answer does not demonstrate credible cross-framework comparison.

followUps.question: Which parts of Spring Boot feel similar to NestJS, and where do they differ meaningfully?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can compare frameworks beyond slogans.

followUps.question: For a team already strong in Laravel or Spring Boot, what would justify moving a new service to NestJS?
followUps.purpose: Probe migration and ecosystem decision reasoning.

en.title: NestJS versus traditional MVC frameworks
en.displayQuestion: What is the biggest difference between NestJS and traditional MVC frameworks such as Laravel or Spring Boot?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate nuanced cross-framework comparison and architecture reasoning.
guidance: Compare architecture, runtime, ecosystem, and degree of batteries-included behavior.
Avoid pretending Spring Boot lacks DI or that Laravel cannot support large systems.
Explain NestJS modules, providers, decorators, and platform-agnostic transport support.
Discuss team expertise and ecosystem fit, not only language preference.
commonMistakes: Calling NestJS just another MVC framework without detail.
Making inaccurate claims about Spring Boot or Laravel.
Comparing only JavaScript, PHP, and Java syntax.
Assuming scalability comes from the framework name alone.

vi.title: NestJS so với framework MVC truyền thống
vi.displayQuestion: Điểm khác biệt lớn nhất giữa NestJS và các framework MVC truyền thống như Laravel hoặc Spring Boot là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng so sánh framework đa chiều và tư duy kiến trúc.
guidance: So sánh architecture, runtime, ecosystem và mức độ batteries-included.
Tránh nói sai rằng Spring Boot không có DI hoặc Laravel không hỗ trợ hệ thống lớn.
Giải thích module, provider, decorator và platform-agnostic transport support của NestJS.
Nói về expertise của team và fit với ecosystem, không chỉ preference ngôn ngữ.
commonMistakes: Gọi NestJS chỉ là một MVC framework khác mà không phân tích.
Claim sai về Spring Boot hoặc Laravel.
Chỉ so sánh syntax JavaScript, PHP và Java.
Giả định scalability đến từ tên framework.

ja.title: NestJS と従来型 MVC framework
ja.displayQuestion: Laravel や Spring Boot のような従来型 MVC framework と NestJS の最大の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: cross-framework comparison と architecture reasoning の深さを評価する。
guidance: architecture、runtime、ecosystem、batteries-included の度合いを比較する。
Spring Boot に DI がない、Laravel が大規模 system を支えられない、など不正確な説明を避ける。
NestJS の modules、providers、decorators、platform-agnostic transport support を説明する。
language preference だけでなく team expertise と ecosystem fit を話す。
commonMistakes: NestJS を単なる別の MVC framework と呼んで詳細を説明しない。
Spring Boot や Laravel について不正確な主張をする。
JavaScript、PHP、Java の syntax だけを比較する。
scalability が framework name だけで決まると考える。

intent: Check whether the candidate knows NestJS GraphQL support and can explain its integration model.
primaryQuestion: Does NestJS support GraphQL, and how does the integration work?

expectedSignals.label: States that NestJS supports GraphQL through @nestjs/graphql and driver integrations.
Mentions Apollo and Mercurius as supported integration paths depending on the platform and driver.
Explains code-first and schema-first approaches.
Connects code-first to TypeScript classes and decorators generating a GraphQL schema.
Connects schema-first to GraphQL SDL files and generated TypeScript definitions.
Mentions resolvers as GraphQL equivalents to request handlers and services/providers as business logic dependencies.
Recognizes GraphQL-specific concerns such as N+1 queries, auth context, schema versioning, and query complexity limits.

redFlags: Says NestJS only supports REST and needs a separate server for GraphQL.
Cannot distinguish resolver from controller responsibilities.
Thinks code-first means no schema exists.
Ignores performance and security concerns unique to GraphQL.

scoringHints.description: Explains @nestjs/graphql, drivers, code-first versus schema-first, resolvers, DI integration, and GraphQL-specific operational concerns.
Understands NestJS GraphQL basics but misses driver details or GraphQL-specific risks.
Knows GraphQL is supported but cannot explain how Nest integrates it.
Answer is incorrect or too generic about GraphQL.

followUps.question: When would you choose code-first versus schema-first GraphQL in a NestJS project?
followUps.purpose: Probe GraphQL architecture choice.

followUps.question: Where would you put authorization and data loading logic in a NestJS GraphQL API?
followUps.purpose: Check resolver, guard, context, and service responsibility boundaries.

en.title: GraphQL integration in NestJS
en.displayQuestion: Does NestJS support GraphQL, and how does the integration work?
en.displayIntent: Check concrete knowledge of NestJS GraphQL module, schema approaches, and resolver architecture.
guidance: Mention @nestjs/graphql and supported driver integrations.
Explain code-first and schema-first clearly.
Map resolvers, services, guards, context, and DTO/input types to GraphQL responsibilities.
Include real GraphQL concerns such as N+1 queries, query complexity, auth, and schema evolution.
commonMistakes: Thinking NestJS GraphQL is separate from the Nest DI system.
Confusing controllers and resolvers without explaining the difference.
Saying code-first means there is no generated schema.
Ignoring GraphQL performance and authorization concerns.

vi.title: Tích hợp GraphQL trong NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: NestJS có hỗ trợ GraphQL không, và nó tích hợp như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết cụ thể về NestJS GraphQL module, cách tiếp cận schema và kiến trúc resolver.
guidance: Nhắc tới @nestjs/graphql và các driver integration được hỗ trợ.
Giải thích rõ code-first và schema-first.
Map resolver, service, guard, context và DTO/input type với trách nhiệm GraphQL.
Bao gồm vấn đề thực tế của GraphQL như N+1 query, query complexity, auth và schema evolution.
commonMistakes: Nghĩ NestJS GraphQL tách khỏi hệ DI của Nest.
Nhầm controller và resolver mà không giải thích khác biệt.
Nói code-first nghĩa là không có schema được tạo ra.
Bỏ qua performance và authorization trong GraphQL.

ja.title: NestJS の GraphQL integration
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS は GraphQL をサポートしていますか。どのように統合されますか。
ja.displayIntent: NestJS GraphQL module、schema approaches、resolver architecture の具体的な知識を確認する。
guidance: @nestjs/graphql と supported driver integrations に触れる。
code-first と schema-first を明確に説明する。
resolvers、services、guards、context、DTO/input types を GraphQL responsibilities に対応させる。
N+1 queries、query complexity、auth、schema evolution など実際の GraphQL concerns を含める。
commonMistakes: NestJS GraphQL が Nest DI system と別物だと思う。
controllers と resolvers の違いを説明せず混同する。
code-first は generated schema がないという意味だと言う。
GraphQL performance と authorization concerns を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: GraphQL quick start
sourceReferences.note: Used for @nestjs/graphql, Apollo/Mercurius drivers, code-first, and schema-first approaches.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for module and provider composition around GraphQL resolvers.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Guards
sourceReferences.note: Used for cross-cutting authorization concepts that apply to request handling.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can explain why NestJS uses decorators and metadata instead of central configuration objects.
primaryQuestion: Why does NestJS use decorators instead of only configuration objects like some other frameworks?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that decorators attach metadata to classes, methods, parameters, or properties.
Describes how Nest uses metadata to build routing maps, register modules and providers, inject dependencies, and apply guards, pipes, interceptors, and filters.
Explains locality: route, validation, authorization, and metadata can live near the code they describe.
Mentions TypeScript and reflection metadata as important parts of the developer experience.
Identifies trade-offs: decorators can hide behavior, depend on metadata conventions, be harder for newcomers, and make some dynamic configuration less explicit.
Avoids claiming decorators are only syntax sugar.

redFlags: Says decorators do nothing except make code shorter.
Cannot explain metadata or how routes are discovered.
Thinks all configuration is impossible in NestJS because decorators exist.
Ignores downsides of metadata-heavy programming.

scoringHints.description: Explains metadata-driven routing and DI, locality of declarations, TypeScript reflection, and decorator trade-offs.
Understands decorators are used for routes and DI but misses metadata details or trade-offs.
Knows decorator syntax but not how Nest uses it internally.
Answer is incorrect or only describes syntax.

followUps.question: What metadata is created by @Controller('users') and @Get(':id'), and how does Nest use it?
followUps.purpose: Check concrete route metadata reasoning.

followUps.question: What is one downside of decorator-heavy code when debugging or onboarding new engineers?
followUps.purpose: Probe balanced framework understanding.

en.title: Decorators and metadata in NestJS
en.displayQuestion: Why does NestJS use decorators instead of only configuration objects like some other frameworks?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate understanding of metadata-driven architecture and decorator trade-offs.
guidance: Explain decorators as metadata attached to classes, methods, parameters, or properties.
Use examples such as @Module, @Controller, @Get, @Injectable, @UseGuards, and parameter decorators.
Connect metadata to route mapping, DI, and cross-cutting concerns.
Discuss downsides such as hidden behavior, reflection dependency, and onboarding difficulty.
commonMistakes: Calling decorators only syntactic sugar.
Missing how Nest discovers routes and providers.
Thinking decorators eliminate all configuration.
Ignoring debugging and readability trade-offs.

vi.title: Decorator và metadata trong NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao NestJS sử dụng decorator thay vì chỉ dùng config object như một số framework khác?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá hiểu biết về kiến trúc dựa trên metadata và trade-off của decorator.
guidance: Giải thích decorator là metadata gắn lên class, method, parameter hoặc property.
Dùng ví dụ như @Module, @Controller, @Get, @Injectable, @UseGuards và parameter decorator.
Liên hệ metadata với route mapping, DI và cross-cutting concern.
Nói về nhược điểm như behavior bị ẩn, phụ thuộc reflection và khó onboarding.
commonMistakes: Gọi decorator chỉ là syntactic sugar.
Không giải thích Nest discover route và provider như thế nào.
Nghĩ decorator loại bỏ mọi config.
Bỏ qua trade-off về debugging và readability.

ja.title: NestJS の decorators と metadata
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS はなぜ一部の framework のような configuration object だけでなく decorators を使うのですか。
ja.displayIntent: metadata-driven architecture と decorator trade-offs の理解を評価する。
guidance: decorators は classes、methods、parameters、properties に付与される metadata だと説明する。
@Module、@Controller、@Get、@Injectable、@UseGuards、parameter decorators などの例を使う。
metadata を route mapping、DI、cross-cutting concerns と結びつける。
hidden behavior、reflection dependency、onboarding difficulty などの欠点も話す。
commonMistakes: decorators を単なる syntactic sugar と呼ぶ。
Nest が routes と providers をどう discover するかを説明しない。
decorators がすべての configuration をなくすと考える。
debugging と readability の trade-off を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Controllers
sourceReferences.note: Used for decorators linking classes with metadata and route mapping.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Custom decorators
sourceReferences.note: Used for decorator mechanics and custom parameter decorators.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Discovery service
sourceReferences.note: Used for runtime metadata inspection context.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands the NestJS application lifecycle from bootstrap to readiness and shutdown hooks.
primaryQuestion: What is the lifecycle of a NestJS application from startup until it is ready to receive requests?

expectedSignals.label: Starts with bootstrap in main.ts using NestFactory.create or createMicroservice with the root AppModule.
Explains that Nest scans modules, builds the module graph, resolves providers, instantiates controllers/providers, and performs dependency injection.
Mentions global setup during bootstrap such as validation pipes, filters, interceptors, guards, CORS, versioning, Swagger, or middleware where relevant.
Mentions lifecycle hooks such as onModuleInit and onApplicationBootstrap before the app starts listening.
States that app.listen or app.init triggers initialization hooks and readiness for handling requests.
Mentions request flow at a high level: middleware, guards, interceptors, pipes, controller/handler, service logic, filters on errors, and response mapping.
Mentions shutdown hooks such as onModuleDestroy, beforeApplicationShutdown, and onApplicationShutdown when shutdown hooks are enabled and app.close or signals occur.

redFlags: Only says main.ts runs and then controllers receive requests.
Does not mention module graph or DI resolution.
Thinks lifecycle hooks run for every request.
Confuses application lifecycle hooks with request-scoped provider lifetime.
Ignores graceful shutdown and resource cleanup.

scoringHints.description: Describes bootstrap, module graph creation, provider resolution, global setup, initialization hooks, listen/init readiness, request pipeline, and shutdown hooks.
Covers bootstrap and readiness but misses shutdown hooks, request pipeline, or module graph detail.
Has a rough mental model but cannot explain DI/lifecycle sequencing.
Answer does not demonstrate practical lifecycle understanding.

followUps.question: Where would you open a database connection or verify a dependency before the app starts accepting traffic?
followUps.purpose: Check readiness and initialization judgment.

followUps.question: Do onModuleInit and onApplicationBootstrap run for every request? Why or why not?
followUps.purpose: Correct confusion between application lifecycle and request lifecycle.

en.title: NestJS application lifecycle
en.displayQuestion: What is the lifecycle of a NestJS application from startup until it is ready to receive requests?
en.displayIntent: Assess lifecycle understanding from bootstrap through initialization, request handling, and shutdown.
guidance: Start from main.ts and NestFactory.create with the root module.
Explain module scanning, dependency graph construction, provider/controller instantiation, and DI resolution.
Mention app-level setup and lifecycle hooks before app.listen or app.init completes.
Include the request pipeline and graceful shutdown hooks at a high level.
commonMistakes: Skipping the module graph and DI container.
Confusing application lifecycle hooks with per-request execution.
Forgetting global pipes, filters, interceptors, middleware, and guards.
Ignoring graceful shutdown and resource cleanup.

vi.title: Lifecycle của ứng dụng NestJS
vi.displayQuestion: Lifecycle của một NestJS application từ lúc khởi động đến lúc sẵn sàng nhận request là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá hiểu biết lifecycle từ bootstrap, initialization, request handling tới shutdown.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ main.ts và NestFactory.create với root module.
Giải thích quét module, dựng dependency graph, instantiate provider/controller và resolve DI.
Nhắc app-level setup và lifecycle hook trước khi app.listen hoặc app.init hoàn tất.
Bao gồm request pipeline và graceful shutdown hook ở mức tổng quan.
commonMistakes: Bỏ qua module graph và DI container.
Nhầm application lifecycle hook với execution theo từng request.
Quên global pipe, filter, interceptor, middleware và guard.
Bỏ qua graceful shutdown và cleanup resource.

ja.title: NestJS application lifecycle
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS application は startup から request を受けられる状態になるまで、どのような lifecycle を辿りますか。
ja.displayIntent: bootstrap、initialization、request handling、shutdown までの lifecycle understanding を評価する。
guidance: main.ts と root module を使った NestFactory.create から始める。
module scanning、dependency graph construction、provider/controller instantiation、DI resolution を説明する。
app.listen または app.init が完了する前の app-level setup と lifecycle hooks に触れる。
request pipeline と graceful shutdown hooks を高レベルで含める。
commonMistakes: module graph と DI container を飛ばす。
application lifecycle hooks と per-request execution を混同する。
global pipes、filters、interceptors、middleware、guards を忘れる。
graceful shutdown と resource cleanup を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Lifecycle events
sourceReferences.note: Used for initialization and shutdown lifecycle hooks.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: First steps
sourceReferences.note: Used for bootstrap and app.listen readiness context.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Request lifecycle
sourceReferences.note: Used for request pipeline ordering and cross-cutting component flow.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can explain NestJS simply and accurately to a non-expert without losing the important technical meaning.
primaryQuestion: How would you explain NestJS to someone who has never heard of it, in two or three sentences?

expectedSignals.label: Gives a concise explanation that NestJS is a framework for building backend/server-side Node.js applications.
Mentions that it gives teams a structured way to organize code using modules, controllers, services/providers, and dependency injection.
Says it can build REST APIs and also supports GraphQL, WebSockets, and microservices when needed.
Explains value in plain language: easier to maintain, test, and scale a codebase across a team.
Avoids overwhelming the listener with jargon or inaccurate simplifications.

redFlags: Cannot explain NestJS without listing many disconnected buzzwords.
Says it is a frontend framework like Angular.
Says it is only an Express wrapper or only a REST generator.
Uses vague claims like 'it makes backend better' without any concrete meaning.

scoringHints.description: Explains NestJS in two or three accurate, accessible sentences covering backend purpose, structure, supported app styles, and maintainability value.
Gives a clear simple answer but misses either supported app styles or concrete structure.
Answer is technically partial or too jargon-heavy for a beginner.
Answer is inaccurate or does not communicate what NestJS is.

followUps.question: Can you rewrite that explanation without using the words 'enterprise', 'scalable', or 'architecture'?
followUps.purpose: Test whether the candidate can communicate concrete meaning without buzzwords.

followUps.question: What is one analogy or example you would use to help a junior developer understand modules and services?
followUps.purpose: Probe teaching ability and conceptual clarity.

en.title: Explain NestJS simply
en.displayQuestion: How would you explain NestJS to someone who has never heard of it, in two or three sentences?
en.displayIntent: Assess concise, accurate communication of a technical framework to a beginner.
guidance: Keep the explanation to two or three sentences.
Say it is for server-side Node.js applications.
Mention structure through modules, controllers, services/providers, and dependency injection.
Explain the practical value in maintainability, testing, and team consistency.
commonMistakes: Using too many framework buzzwords.
Making it sound like a frontend framework.
Reducing NestJS to an Express wrapper only.
Forgetting to say why a team would use it.

vi.title: Giải thích NestJS một cách đơn giản
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ giải thích NestJS cho một người chưa biết gì về nó như thế nào, trong 2-3 câu?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng giao tiếp ngắn gọn, chính xác về framework kỹ thuật cho người mới.
guidance: Giữ câu trả lời trong 2-3 câu.
Nói đây là framework cho ứng dụng server-side Node.js.
Nhắc tới cấu trúc qua module, controller, service/provider và dependency injection.
Giải thích giá trị thực tế ở maintainability, testing và tính nhất quán trong team.
commonMistakes: Dùng quá nhiều buzzword framework.
Làm nó nghe như frontend framework.
Thu hẹp NestJS thành chỉ Express wrapper.
Quên nói vì sao team nên dùng nó.

ja.title: NestJS を簡単に説明する
ja.displayQuestion: NestJS をまったく知らない人に、二、三文でどのように説明しますか。
ja.displayIntent: 初心者に technical framework を短く正確に説明できるか評価する。
guidance: 説明は二、三文に収める。
server-side Node.js applications のための framework だと述べる。
modules、controllers、services/providers、dependency injection による structure に触れる。
maintainability、testing、team consistency という実用的価値を説明する。
commonMistakes: framework buzzword を使いすぎる。
frontend framework のように聞こえる説明にする。
NestJS を Express wrapper だけに縮小する。
team がなぜ使うのかを言い忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Documentation overview
sourceReferences.note: Used for concise framework definition and conventional structure.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Modules
sourceReferences.note: Used for explaining modules and application organization.

sourceReferences.label: NestJS docs: Platform agnosticism
sourceReferences.note: Used for support across HTTP frameworks, WebSockets, and microservices.
